item.title: Could 3D Code Editors Work?
item.description: A poetic exploration of a visual programming language that leverages event‑emitters and AI to generate 3D space‑station style code maps, arguing that such interfaces will be the next frontier for AI‑driven development.

item.title: The Return Of Morgana And The Knights Of The Shattered Round
item.description: The post argues that humans possess latent “meta‑powers,” likening one of them to a “pantheon” – a memory‑palace system for organizing knowledge. It critiques how religions have eroded these archetypal legends, invoking Merlin, Morgana, King Arthur, and Sir Galahad as symbols of valor, honor, and the untarnished self. The author suggests that restoring the pantheon requires shedding inner conflict, religious “stains,” and false narratives. By embracing wisdom, song, Joseph Campbell’s archetypes, Kurt Vonnegut’s story shapes, and philosophical insight, individuals can awaken their inner knights and revive the mythic “Avalon” within a modern age.

item.title: A Call For Young Power Programmers
item.description: This post describes a vision of AI as a scalable creative and production system rather than a one-off assistant, where the real power comes from structuring workflows: using example-driven prompting, batch processing, and containerized AI services (e.g., exposing models through an OpenAPI layer inside Docker) to generate large volumes of coherent output instead of single responses. It explores extending AI into domains like music production, where instead of generating one song you generate entire albums through structured project files, allowing models to expand creative intent in unexpected ways. It also emphasizes building AI systems with visualization in mind from the start—especially 3D interfaces using tools like three.js—so that complex generative systems can be understood spatially rather than textually.

item.title: Homemade Lemonade
item.description: Frustrated but inventive journey using two AIs—one that generates lyrics from broad ideas and another that composes and performs—to push back against a music industry that packages one strong track with filler. Early experiments produced AI country songs mixing workout advice and blunt imagery; a faulty run unexpectedly produced 36 tracks with the same lyrics but varied instrumentation and voices, revealing how production can create distinct experiences from identical words. After iterative fixing with a “savant” model author trained through many simple prompts, he achieved a satisfactory batch of country songs that blend storytelling, drums, and adventurous themes like Galahad, demonstrating a creative workflow where AI idea-seeding yields more intriguing results than handing it finished lyrics.

item.title: On Easily Understanding The Complexity Of Artificial Intelligence Generated Applications
item.description: I am advocating the creation of “temporary” satellite applications that wrap around a complex AI codebase, providing concise visual summaries of its many features. Though not essential to the language itself, these lightweight GUIs act as ecological interface designs—modern, blinking‑light abstractions that map system structures onto intuitive dashboards, revealing hidden functionality, priority work, and areas needing improvement. This approach mirrors the historic art of Ecological Interface Design, offering an independent yet parallel method for taming complexity through external, temporary visual lenses.

item.title: Nineteen Ninety One
item.description: A dying architect of systemic oppression explains how poverty erodes human agency and solidarity, yet the narrator concludes that AI and democratization of knowledge now threatens to dismantle the enduring machinery of control.

item.title: Fresh Country Workout
item.description: The world has changed. Start creating your own music with artificial intelligence.

item.title: Coding Agents Are A Silly Idea, and Programmers Are Silly Too
item.description: The author argues that programmers already embody AI through their use of logic and that current AI mainly amplifies traditional programming rather than replacing its core. He critiques poor education and tooling, and propose building a rich Process Library plus two flagship desktop apps: (1) a Process Library browser (an agent-extension manager with meta-processes) and (2) a drag-and-drop UI builder with 500+ reusable components. Coding agents should be used to bootstrap such infrastructure, not for day-to-day low-level coding. With these tools, developers can rapidly create complex, event-sourced desktop applications and even compose higher-level systems (e.g., a genuine learning-focused university) that foster creativity and deep learning.

item.title: Use Artificial Intelligence To Build And Sell Useful Applications
item.description: The post argues that while large AI models have advantages, smaller, specialized models with clear instructions can be more practical and cost-effective for specific tasks, emphasizing the importance of niche markets and efficient software development.

item.title: Semantic Mass: Most Applications Do Not Know What Anything Means
item.description: Software architecture should move away from manual UI construction toward a model where user interfaces emerge as natural projections of an underlying symbolic world built on semantic meaning.

item.title: Buff Ladies
item.description: Through a narrative about women using a fluid, progressive, and rhythmic weightlifting technique, the author reflects on the importance of continuous movement and suggests that the ultimate shortcut to fitness is found through outdoor adventure.

item.title: Grow Your Own Local Super Intelligence: It Is Just Step By Step Procedures In Text Files
item.description: The author contends that local AI can become a superintelligence by executing a curated library of procedures that are generated and refined using large AI models.

item.title: Learnings From A Hard Week With Artificial Intelligence
item.description: The author explores a methodology for using XML as a structural blueprint to orchestrate an AI-driven code-translation "assembly line," while arguing that local AI is essential for maintaining control, format, and reliability.

item.title: Your Own Artificial Intelligence
item.description: By leveraging modern large language models to automate the configuration and knowledge building of "Good Old Fashioned AI" (GOFAI), developers can create powerful, efficient, and specialized local expert systems for tasks such as code generation and procedural user interface design.

item.title: The Building Of The AI City; Or, A Touch Of Gibberish With An Invitation Into Programming
item.description: The author describes an innovative AI management system using a city map interface where virtual corporations collaborate and self-improve to overcome the memory limitations of individual AI models.

item.title: The City
item.description: The post proposes a new technological paradigm that integrates XML, MOO, and LLMs into a navigable, city-like language designed to democratize software development and empower anyone to build the future through structured, spatial, and conversational intelligence.

item.title: The Call Of Defeat
item.description: This polemic critiques the systemic devaluation of human life by profit-driven institutions and challenges the individual to resist this dehumanization through intellectual resilience and the pursuit of greatness.

item.title: Super Short Fitness Advice For Extremely Busy Ladies
item.description: To build muscle and strength, start with light, continuous dumbbell exercises to maximize and expand your endurance, then gradually increase both the weight and the duration of your workouts as your body adapts.

item.title: You Must Program AI With XML
item.description: The author argues that XML is superior to JSON and Markdown because its readable, structural nature provides an ideal declarative blueprint for AI-driven application development and complex system management.

item.title: A Day Of Tinkering
item.description: The author describes extensive local AI runs (up to 12 h) that outperform paid cloud solutions and enable rapid development of complex projects, arguing that only local AI can be truly specialized. He advocates a declarative, XML‑based approach where XML serves as both data format and abstract syntax tree, leaving imperative code to AI.

item.title: Fixing Code Liability: Here Is How You Can Make Your Software Product More Valuable
item.description: To mitigate the liability of code maintenance, developers should shift from selling standalone software to providing modular components integrated within custom AI agent harnesses that automate upkeep and security.

item.title: Basement Apartment
item.description: A political figure and her husband plot a calculated campaign to manipulate the masses through manufactured resentment, only to realize that someone has sabotaged their strategy to prevent them from reaching their goal.

item.title: Let AI Do The Programming
item.description: The post argues that instead of learning traditional programming, individuals should learn to use procedural instructions to command AI agents to autonomously execute complex tasks like UI development and real-time content generation.

item.title: Big Problems With Little AI
item.description: The key to leveraging AI for complex software development is to use architectural principles to break down large, complex systems into small, discrete, and well-documented components that small-scale AI can easily manage and assemble.

item.title: Close Encounters
item.description: The essay explores the hypothesis that the gravitational influence of ancient globular clusters passing near our solar system could periodically perturb the Oort cloud, potentially triggering periods of cometary infall that impact Earth's biological history.

item.title: From John
item.description: John issues a profound call to resist the invisible tyrannies of debt, distraction, and conformity by guarding the sovereignty of the mind and committing to the persistent, courageous pursuit of truth, wisdom, and human greatness.

item.title: Operating Systems: You Already Know Application Architecture
item.description: The post proposes a unified application architecture built on the principle that "everything is a file," utilizing an XML-based virtual file system to manage data, processes, and reactive computations through a universal, path-based interface.

item.title: A User Interface For Local AI, And The End Of World Poverty
item.description: The author proposes a vision for a decentralized, local AI "political system" composed of specialized, autonomous agents that follow encoded procedures to manage complex, multi-tenant business ecosystems, sales management, and application creation.

item.title: Zweihänder - Six Feet o' Singin' Steel
item.description: In a spirited and insult-laden poem, a young Scottish woman passionately defends the unmatched superiority of the massive Zweihänder sword over all other weapons.

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Will Never Forgive Me for Asking It to Perform Such Wonders
item.description: The author describes the transformative power of using state-of-the-art AI to rapidly develop a complex, feature-rich web design system in a fraction of the time traditional programming would require.

item.title: Read In Case Of Getting Surprise Knighted By A Woodland Creature
item.description: This poetic text celebrates the "knighting" of individuals as a call to embrace radical independence, intellectual self-education, and the pursuit of authenticity to become an unstoppable force of nature.

item.title: Super Quick Intro To Programming With AI
item.description: The author proposes using a Zooming User Interface (ZUI) and a generic Web-API-like windowing system to create decoupled, zoomable control panels that serve as an interference-free layer for AI-driven web development and automation.

item.title: Into Web Development: The Three Strange Mice
item.description: The author explores how AI-driven development enables the rapid creation of specialized web design tools and immersive, skeuomorphic themes within a custom content management system.

item.title: Tyger! Tyger!
item.description: This manifesto responds to William Blake's Tyger and urges readers to reject societal indoctrination and reclaim their individual agency through independent thought and self-determined greatness, for peace, wisdom, and the betterment of the world.

item.title: Do Not Sit At The Gym; Or, How Does It Work Anyway?
item.description: To achieve true physical rejuvenation and avoid plateaus, one must gradually and courageously increase physical challenges—moving from walking to dancing to lifting—allowing the body to adapt through consistent, incremental progression.

item.title: Ozymandias
item.description: An immortal being who has lived through millennia reflects on the cycles of human greatness and loss, finding hope in the enduring human instinct to look upward and explore.

item.title: The Husbandry of the Grail
item.description: Sir Galahad discovers the sinister plan to manipulate and diminish humanity through the Grail's power, a deceptive instrument of manipulation and despair, ultimately knighting those who stand for wisdom, and inspiring them to reject false idols and false and hollow authority, and to seek greatness through critical, unyielding, undying thought.

item.title: Reversing Model View Controller
item.description: The author argues that a structured implementation of the MVC pattern—using an XML-based model as an application blueprint and a command-driven controller—is the essential framework for leveraging AI to build and manage complex software applications.

item.title: Unfounded Observations On The Subject Of Artificial Intelligence
item.description: The author argues that the true potential of local AI will be realized not by increasing model size, but by surrounding smaller models with specialized software and multi-agent frameworks that function as a virtual management office to coordinate complex tasks and ensure quality.

item.title: The Building Of The Genius Savant
item.description: To achieve true autonomy and bypass exploitative societal structures, individuals must master AI through daily practice and the development of modular, agent-based programming systems.

item.title: The Age Of Code
item.description: The author demonstrates how AI can be used to rapidly develop complex software ecosystems, arguing that the technology empowers individual developers to achieve massive productivity gains that traditional corporate metrics fail to capture.

item.title: Miniature Autonomous Companies
item.description: The author proposes building "autonomous companies" managed by AI agents that sell UI templates as foundational blueprints for AI-driven application development, focusing on the practice of entrepreneurship rather than immediate profit.

item.title: Use Big Ideas: Speak To Your Programming Artificial Intelligence By Subtle Analogy
item.description: The author suggests that instead of using technical, variable-heavy programming instructions, one can more effectively guide AI by using real-world metaphors and structured XML blueprints to manage complex application designs.

item.title: Programming Language Design: Syntactic Sugar Over Data Streams For Ladies
item.description: The author proposes transitioning from traditional line-by-line programming to a reactive, stream-based paradigm that treats data as continuous pipelines to better manage the asynchronous nature of modern information flows.

item.title: XML For Girls Learning Programming
item.description: The post explores the potential of using AI to leverage XML's structural self-similarity to design complex, programmable architectures and formal language specifications.

item.title: Fake Education Is A Dead End
item.description: The text advocates for reclaiming one's potential through self-directed learning and the use of AI in programming to build independent, automated systems while escaping the restrictive stresses of traditional education and societal exploitation.

item.title: The Year Of The New Design Portfolio
item.description: I’ve been experimenting with AI‑assisted web design—from generating style guides and Bootstrap themes to automating Docker setups and visual programming—while building a self‑maintaining product ecosystem that blends creative UI components, symbolic coding, and future autonomous agents.

item.title: Woodland Adventures Are Not Scary
item.description: The post reflects on various personal encounters and observations of nature's unpredictable, strange, and beautiful elements, ranging from mysterious fox cries and flash floods to the well-behaved raccoons of the Nordhouse Dunes.

item.title: The Autonomous Agent Afternoon
item.description: The author describes their experience deploying an autonomous AI agent to independently implement software improvements and highlights the agent's potential for continuous self-improvement and learning their personal coding style.

item.title: Bodybuilding Is Easy; But, Unbelievable
item.description: The author contends that true bodybuilding is achieved through rhythmic, low‑weight eventually-heavier dumbbell routines combined with dance‑like motions, favoring gradual muscle adaptation over a year rather than heavy lifting which can only lead to a disastrous plateau.
